title: dendranthema zawadzkii var. campanulatum
intro: Dendranthema zawadzkii var. campanulatum, also known as T'ung-hua, is a plant species within the daisy family, Asteraceae. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to reduce inflammation, and is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and colds. The plant is rich in sesquiterpene lactones, which are responsible for its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, providing health benefits such as pain relief, improved digestion, and enhanced immune function. In traditional Chinese medicine, Dendranthema zawadzkii var. campanulatum is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, and is also used in herbal remedies, such as infusions and capsules.
